query: How much chemical should I put in my sprayer to cover 40 acres, I'm using them new saddle tanks and I want to make sure I'm gettin' the right amount out?
gold_answer: With two 200-gallon saddle tanks at the example band rate (1/2 lb Anychem 3 + 1/2 pt Anychem 4 per acre), one tank capacity covers 40 acres per refill, requiring 20 lb Anychem 3 and 20 pts Anychem 4.
gold_source: soybeans recommended chemicals for weed and brush control
gold_snippet: gal tank capacity = 40 acres per refill
set_aside_reason: Derived computation (tank capacity -> acres/refill); not a verbatim corpus fact, containment judge cannot fairly verify.

query: I'm thinkin' of switchin' to them new LED bulbs in my chicken barn, but I'm worried about 'em overheatin', will they burn out quick or is there a way they can cool themselves down?
gold_answer: LED bulbs do produce heat when the diodes are on, but far less than an incandescent bulb.
gold_source: poultry fsa8005 poultry lighting led bulbs provide energy savings and durability
gold_snippet: When the diodes are on, heat is produced. While far less than the heat of an incandescent bulb, this heat

query: How many seeds per acre should I plant for my rice crop, and when's the best time to put 'em in the ground to get a good yield?
set_aside_reason: Chunk is a bibliography (Counce; Faw & Johnston seeding-date refs); seeding-rate/date answer is elsewhere in the rice handbook — human should check the full source.

query: I got a bunch of new pine seedlings and there's a lot of weeds comin' up around 'em, what's the best time to spray somethin' to kill them weeds without hurtin' my pines?
gold_answer: Spray Oust XP (sulfometuron 0.06 lb/A) in the late dormant season, soon after transplanting while seedlings are dormant; applying after bud break or leaf-out can injure them.
gold_source: soybeans recommended chemicals for weed and brush control
gold_snippet:
Application after bud break
or leaf-out may cause injury to the hardwoods.

query: How much nitrogen fertilizer should I put on my loamy field to get a good corn crop this year?
I'm having trouble with stink bugs in my rice field, how many should I expect to see before I need to spray something to control them?
gold_answer: Rice stink bug threshold: 5 per 10 sweeps in weeks 1-2 after 75% heading, and 10 per 10 sweeps in weeks 3-4.
gold_source: rice 2020 br wells arkansas rice research studies
gold_snippet:
For weeks 3 and 4 after 75% heading, our threshold
is 10 RSB per 10 sweeps.

query: I got a field with them pesky horseweeds and wild garlic, what's a good mix to spray on 'em before my wheat gets too tall?
gold_answer: Harmony Extra 50 SG + Quelex 20 DF at 0.75 oz/A each controls horseweed, wild garlic and other broadleaves; apply before flag leaf emergence.
gold_source: soybeans recommended chemicals for weed and brush control
gold_snippet: Apply before flag leaf emergence.

query: I'm gettin' low yields on my soybeans, what kind of fertilizer do I need to add to get 'em to grow better?
gold_answer: Soybeans take up about 5.0 lb N, 1.0 lb P2O5 and 3.8 lb K2O per bushel produced; fertilize P and K to match (soybean P/K demand is 1.5-3x that of corn or rice).
gold_source: soybeans ch 5 fertilization and liming practices feb 2013
gold_snippet:
total aboveground uptake of 5.0 lb N, 1.0 lb P2O5
and 3.8 lb K2O required to produce each bushel of
soybeans.

set_aside_reason: Research-study abstract comparing Highcard/Max-Ace vs Provisia programs; no actionable recommendation to transcribe.

query: How long after I plant my crop can I expect it to come up and when should I be ready to flood my fields?
set_aside_reason: Seeding/emergence/flood-date table whose numeric values were lost in OCR; only headers remain.

query: I sprayed my pond for weeds, but it didn't work. Could the muddy water be why it didn't kill 'em?
gold_answer: Yes - in muddy water diquat binds to the suspended particles and is rendered inactive, so it won't control the weeds.
gold_source: rice recommended chemicals for weed and brush control
gold_snippet: Diquat binds with suspended particles, rendering it inactive.

query: How much should I be makin' per acre after payin' for all my expenses, equipment, and overhead to know if my farm's doin' alright?
gold_answer: Example rice enterprise budget: total operating expenses $627.90/ac, returns to operating expenses $188.10/ac, total specified expenses $750.28/ac.
gold_source: rice 2019 br wells arkansas rice research studies
gold_snippet: Total Specified Expenses
set_aside_reason: gold_snippet 'Total Specified Expenses' is a generic table header, not the net-return $/acre the query asks; needs re-transcription of the actual figure.

query: I'm fixin' to plant soybeans, how many seeds per acre do I really need to get a good crop without wastin' money on extra seed?
gold_answer: Non-irrigated soybean yield differed little across seeding rates from 60,000 plants/A (~30 lb seed/A) up to 240,000 plants/A (~120 lb seed/A); stay near Extension-recommended rates rather than overspending on seed.
gold_source: soybeans ch 7 planting practices
gold_snippet:
seeding rates varying from
60,000 (approximately 30 lbs of seed/A) up to
240,000 plants/A (120 lbs of seed/A)

query: My turkey flock's been actin' poorly, weak and slow to grow, what's the best way to check if it's somethin' in the water they're drinkin'?
gold_answer: Test the drinking water: high nitrates (~1-5 mg/l) cause poor growth and feed conversion and can indicate fecal contamination (also test bacteria); also test lead (.014 mg/l limit), which long-term causes weak bones and fertility problems.
gold_source: poultry interpreting water quality results
gold_snippet:
High nitrate levels can result in poor growth
and feed conversions.

query: I got a field overrun with them pesky broadleaf weeds and brush, what's a good chemical to spray to get rid of 'em?
gold_answer: Triclopyr (Garlon 3A/4 or Tahoe 3A) at 1.0-4.5 lb/A plus nonionic surfactant controls many annual and perennial broadleaf weeds and brush; apply postemergence during the growing season.
gold_source: soybeans recommended chemicals for weed and brush control
gold_snippet: triclopyr @ 1.0 to 4.5 lb/A

query: My chickens are gettin' close to market size, how can I keep 'em healthy and safe from predators and bad weather?
gold_answer: Keep near-market chickens in environmentally controlled barns that protect them from predators, disease agents and environmental extremes.
gold_source: poultry fsa8007 hormones in our poultry is it for real
gold_snippet:
Chickens near market age benefit from a life
free of predators, disease agents and environmental extremes

query: I got a bunch of chickens with sores and scabs, and I'm worried they're gonna infect the rest, how can I keep it from spreadin' to my healthy birds?
gold_answer: Avian pox virus survives in dry scabs, feathers and skin dander and spreads to healthy birds through skin cuts and on hands/clothing; isolate infected birds and avoid carrying scab material to healthy ones.
gold_source: poultry fsa8008 avian pox in small flocks
gold_snippet:
The
avian pox virus is highly resistant in
the dry scabs from recovering birds
and may be easily transmitted to
uninfected birds.

query: I'm havin' trouble gettin' good yields from my hybrid rice crop, what makes some rice varieties better suited for hybrid production than others?
gold_answer: Successful hybrid rice production needs traits such as stigma size and high pollen load, which are not present in all rice varieties.
gold_source: rice 2021 br wells arkansas rice research studies
gold_snippet:
high pollen load are needed for successful hybrid rice
production, which may not be prevalent in all rice varieties.

query: I'm growin' Clearfield rice, can I use Beyond Xtra without worryin' about what I plant next year?
gold_answer: A Beyond Xtra-only program (Clearfield varieties only) removes the rotation restriction, allowing conventional or Provisia/Max-Ace rice to be planted the following year.
gold_source: soybeans recommended chemicals for weed and brush control
gold_snippet: Use on Clearfield rice varieties only.

query: I'm having trouble with some of my rice crops, they're not yielding like they used to, what can I do to figure out what's causing the problem and find a better variety to plant?
set_aside_reason: GWAS methods abstract (SNP filtering, FarmCPU); no actionable variety-selection advice to transcribe.

query: My rice crop's yield is sufferin' near them pigweeds, what's the best time to take care of 'em so they don't hurt my harvest?
gold_answer: Palmer amaranth (pigweed) can cut rice yield by more than 80% within 6 inches of the weed; its emergence timing relative to rice is crucial, so control it early.
gold_source: rice 2023 br wells arkansas rice research studies
gold_snippet: rice yield by more than 80% within 6 inches from the weed.

query: My rice is gettin' hurt when it's hot and dry, especially when it's bloomin' and the grains are formmin', what can I do to help it?
set_aside_reason: High-night-temperature phenotyping methods abstract; no mitigation advice to transcribe.

query: How can I save water when irrigating my rice fields, I'm lookin' for ways to cut back without hurtin' my crop?
gold_answer: Water-saving rice irrigation methods include furrow irrigation, MIRI (multiple-inlet rice irrigation) and using soil-moisture sensors.
gold_source: rice 2023 br wells arkansas rice research studies
gold_snippet: Another water-saving method of rice irrigation is MIRI.

query: I'm having trouble with blight on my rice crop, what's the best time to spray fungicide to get rid of it?
set_aside_reason: Research-funding list (project titles + dollar amounts); fungicide-timing answer is not actually given.

query: I'm having trouble with my rice crop, some areas didn't come up right after planting, what's the best time to plant to get a good stand?

query: I got a field with some pretty dense weeds comin' up, what's the best way to get rid of 'em without hurtin' my cotton that's just startin' to grow?
gold_answer: Rate the herbicide by soil texture: 0.8 pt/A coarse, 1.6 pt/A medium, 2.4 pt/A fine soil (Linuron/Linex 4L 0.5-1.5 lb/A); apply after cotton is 15 inches tall.
gold_source: rice recommended chemicals for weed and brush control
gold_snippet:
Coarse Soil (light) - 0.8 pt/A
Medium Soil - 1.6 pt/A
Fine Soil (heavy) - 2.4 pt/A

query: I'm seein' some worms eatin' away at my rice crop, causin' some damage to the leaves and stems, how many of 'em do I need to see before I should spray somethin' to get rid of 'em?
gold_answer: Treat armyworms in rice when there are six or more per square foot; later in the season treat when fall armyworms are present and damaging the flag leaf.
gold_source: rice arkansas rice production handbook
gold_snippet:
Treat when six or more
armyworms per square foot.

query: How can I tell if the difference in yield between two types of corn I'm growin' is real or just luck?
gold_answer: Use the LSD (0.05) value: if two varieties differ by more than the LSD for that column, the difference is real (5% chance it's random); a smaller difference is likely luck.
gold_source: soybeans 2025 arkansas soybean update
gold_snippet:
the minimum yield difference required between two varieties within the same location
before concluding that their yields are truly different

query: How early can I start plantin' rice this year, considerin' all the new seed treatments and equipment I've got?
gold_answer: Thanks to better grain-drill seed placement and fungicide/insecticide seed treatments, rice can now be planted earlier than the old 11 April early date (window ~11 April-9 May).
gold_source: rice 2022 br wells arkansas rice research studies
gold_snippet:
rice planting in Arkansas today can
be much earlier than the early date used by Griffin and Kelley in
2011 (11 April)

query: I got a bunch of them yellow-flowered weeds poppin' up in my fields, how can I get rid of 'em without hurtin' my other plants?
gold_answer: For dandelion and similar yellow-flowered weeds, spray glyphosate and repeat when regrowth appears (takes most of the summer), keeping spray off non-target plants; spot MSMA is legal on sod farms and golf courses.

query: Which corn seed should I plant to get the best yield on my land, I'm lookin' for somethin' that'll give me a good harvest?
I planted soybeans next to my rice field, will the spray I use on the soybeans hurt my rice crop?
gold_answer: Yes - there is a risk of rice injury from herbicide drift and carryover from adjacent soybean fields (e.g., DFF/diflufenican drift); research is ongoing, so spray with caution near rice.
gold_source: rice 2022 br wells arkansas rice research studies
gold_snippet:
the risk for rice injury caused by DFF drift from adjacent soybean
fields

Performance of selected Clearfield medium-grain experimental lines and check varieties in
Clearfield

query: I got some soybean fields where the plants are gettin' burned after I sprayed for weeds, what varieties can handle that metribuzin stuff without gettin' hurt?
gold_answer: The 2025 metribuzin tolerance test ranks soybean varieties by injury at 0.5 lb ai/ac (e.g. Moderate: AG45XF3, AG46XF3, AG48XF2); pick varieties rated Slight for best tolerance.

gold_snippet: 2025 Soybean varieties that tested Moderate injury to metribuzin at 0.5 lb ai/ac.
